response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings a strong and genuinely broad individual contributor background spanning 15+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and systems domains, with pockets of relevant developer tooling work including CI/CD infrastructure, test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and MCP-based agentic tooling at Google. However, this is a full-time Level II people management role, and the resume contains zero evidence of managing engineers — no direct reports, no hiring, no performance reviews, no organizational leadership. Every position listed is an IC role, including his current Staff Software Engineer title at Google. The core competency this role is being filled for is absent, making him a poor fit regardless of his technical depth.
strengths: 15+ years of software development well above the 8-year minimum, with genuine breadth across stacks and domains

Directly relevant developer tooling experience: CI/CD infrastructure setup at Altom, open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial runner), and MCP code generators supporting agentic workflows at Google
Staff-level seniority at Google with cross-functional technical scope, demonstrating the technical credibility needed to guide system designs and code reviews
concerns: No people management experience of any kind — no direct reports, no hiring, no performance conversations, no 1:1 ownership mentioned at any point in a 15-year career
Career trajectory is exclusively individual contributor; there is no evidence of the 3 years of technical leadership or organizational change management the role requires
Mobile platform depth is shallow and incidental — brief iOS and Apple TV work at WatchUp, insufficient for a role anchored in a major mobile platform ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Relevant developer tooling and productivity work
No organizational or team leadership track record
